---
title: "Week 3 — One-variable summaries"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

What does one variable look like?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Categorical and quantitative variables, frequency tables, and the
core visual summaries.

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

Describe the shape, center, spread, and unusual values of a given
graph or summary table.

### Friday — quiz / application

Quiz mirroring Wednesday's micro-lab on a new scenario; short
graph-interpretation application.

## Primary source basis

OpenIntro IMS Ch 4 (explore-categorical) and Ch 5 (explore-numerical).

## Supplemental source / custom needs

Health/life-science graph and table examples; no R required for
students.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Choose appropriate summaries for categorical and quantitative
      variables.
- [ ] Describe shape, center, spread, and unusual values.
- [ ] Avoid overinterpreting a single graph.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Micro-lab ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
